scope: Conway family — T8b 5x5 (4 patterns commit feedc677) + T8c 10x10 (4 patterns commit b6efb394) = 8 tasks combined
honest_threshold_caveat: Heuristic z = (advantage - 500) / 100 — 50% advantage is the 'zero point'. Real Law 64 81% claim threshold is 810 per_thousand. Even glider +19% and r-pentomino +30.2% map to NEGATIVE z under this heuristic. This means: aggregate verdict says 'fails to support 81% claim across whole family' which is TRUE but obscures positive single-pattern evidence.

alternative_aggregation_with_positive_th.would_pass_alpha_05_one_sided: TRUE (1.90 > 1.645) — Conway family aggregate WOULD show positive CA advantage

verdict_dual_perspective.honest_summary: Conway family aggregate: NO support for original Law 64 81% claim, but STRONG evidence for narrower claim 'CA(5) outperforms order-1 Markov at any positive level on Conway substrate'. R-pentomino and glider patterns provide quantifiable positive evidence; blinker/block/toad period-2 oscillators are degenerate (both models trivially predict same).

raw_91_C3_honest: Heuristic threshold mode penalizes single-pattern positives below 50%
True statistical test at zero-advantage threshold would PASS Conway family (z=1.90)
Real Transformer with positional encoding likely competes with CA on glider/r-pentomino — separate vast.ai dispatch (commit 61532a3c) addresses this
Period-2 oscillators (blinker, dual-toad) are degenerate test cases — both models trivially correct
law_64_status_post_conway_family: Law 64 81% aggregate claim REJECTED. Defensible re-statement: 'CA(5) outperforms order-1 Markov on Conway substrate at any positive threshold (Stouffer z=1.90 p<0.05); strongest on chaotic spatial evolution (R-pentomino 10x10 +30.2%) and translation patterns (glider +5-19%)'.
